modules.name: ee.ee_array
documentation: A wrapper for Arrays.

classes.name: ee.ee_array.Array

methods.name: ee.ee_array.Array.__init__
documentation:
Creates an Array wrapper.

Returns an Array with the given coordinates.

Args:
  values: An existing array to cast, or a number/list of numbers/nested list
    of numbers of any depth to create an array from. For nested lists, all
    inner arrays at the same depth must have the same length, and numbers
    may only be present at the deepest level.
  pixelType: The type of each number in the values argument. If the pixel
    type is not provided, it will be inferred from the numbers in 'values'.
    If there aren't any numbers in 'values', this type must be provided.

methods.name: ee.ee_array.Array.initialize
documentation: Imports API functions to this class.

methods.name: ee.ee_array.Array.reset
documentation: Removes imported API functions from this class.

methods.name: ee.ee_array.Array.abs
documentation: On an element-wise basis, computes the absolute value of the input.

methods.name: ee.ee_array.Array.accum
documentation:
Accumulates elements of an array along the given axis.

Sets each element of the result to the reduction of elements along that axis
up to and including the current position.

May be used to make a cumulative sum, a monotonically increasing sequence,
etc.

Args:
  axis: Axis along which to perform the accumulation.
  reducer: Reducer to accumulate values. Default is SUM, to produce the
    cumulative sum of each vector along the given axis.

Returns:
  An ee.Array.

methods.name: ee.ee_array.Array.acos
documentation: Computes the arccosine in radians of the input.

methods.name: ee.ee_array.Array.add
documentation:
On an element-wise basis, adds the first value to the second.

Args:
  right: The values to add.

Returns:
  An ee.Array.

methods.name: ee.ee_array.Array.And
documentation:
Returns 1 if both values are non-zero.

Args:
  right: The values to add.

Returns:
  An ee.Array.

methods.name: ee.ee_array.Array.argmax
documentation:
Returns the position of the maximum value in an array.

If there are multiple occurrences of the maximum, returns the position of
the first.

Returns:
  An ee.List that is empty if the input in an empty ee.Array.

methods.name: ee.ee_array.Array.asin
documentation: Computes the arcsine in radians of the input.

methods.name: ee.ee_array.Array.atan
documentation: Computes the arctangent in radians of the input.

methods.name: ee.ee_array.Array.atan2
documentation:
Calculates the angle formed by the 2D vector [x, y].

Args:
  right: The y values.

Returns:
  An ee.Array.

methods.name: ee.ee_array.Array.bitCount
documentation:
Calculates the number of one-bits.

Uses a 64-bit two's complement binary representation of the input.

Returns:
  An ee.Array.

methods.name: ee.ee_array.Array.bitsToArray
documentation:
Returns an Array from the bits of an integer.

The array has as many elements as the position of the highest set bit, or a
single 0 for a value of 0.

Args:
  input: The integer to transform.

methods.name: ee.ee_array.Array.bitwiseAnd
documentation:
On an element-wise basis, calculates the bitwise AND of the input values.

Args:
  right: The values to AND with.

Returns:
  An ee.Array.

methods.name: ee.ee_array.Array.bitwiseNot
documentation:
On an element-wise basis, calculates the bitwise NOT of the input.

In the smallest signed integer type that can hold the input.

Returns:
  An ee.Array.

methods.name: ee.ee_array.Array.bitwiseOr
documentation:
On an element-wise basis, calculates the bitwise OR of the input values.

Args:
  right: The values to OR with.

Returns:
  An ee.Array.

methods.name: ee.ee_array.Array.bitwiseXor
documentation:
On an element-wise basis, calculates the bitwise XOR of the input values.

Args:
  right: The values to XOR with.

Returns:
  An ee.Array.

methods.name: ee.ee_array.Array.byte
documentation: Casts the input value to an unsigned 8-bit integer.

methods.name: ee.ee_array.Array.cat
documentation:
Returns an Array that is the concatenation of the given arrays.

Concatenates multiple arrays into a single array along the given axis. Each
array must have the same dimensionality and the same length on all axes
except the concatenation axis.

Args:
  arrays: Arrays to concatenate.
  axis: Axis to concatenate along. Defaults to 0.

methods.name: ee.ee_array.Array.cbrt
documentation: On an element-wise basis, computes the cubic root of the input.

methods.name: ee.ee_array.Array.ceil
documentation: Computes the smallest integer greater than or equal to the input.

methods.name: ee.ee_array.Array.cos
documentation: On an element-wise basis, computes the cosine of the input in radians.

methods.name: ee.ee_array.Array.cosh
documentation: On an element-wise basis, computes the hyperbolic cosine of the input.

methods.name: ee.ee_array.Array.cut
documentation:
Cut an array along one or more axes.

Args:
  position: Cut an array along one or more axes. The positions args
    specifies either a single value for each axis of the array, or -1,
    indicating the whole axis. The output will be an array that has the same
    dimensions as the input, with a length of 1 on each axis that was not -1
    in the positions array.

Returns:
  An ee.Array.

methods.name: ee.ee_array.Array.digamma
documentation: On an element-wise basis, computes the digamma function of the input.

methods.name: ee.ee_array.Array.divide
documentation:
Divides the first value by the second, returning 0 for division by 0.

Args:
  right: The values to divide by.

Returns:
  An ee.Array.

methods.name: ee.ee_array.Array.dotProduct
documentation:
Compute the dot product between two 1-D arrays.

Args:
  array2: The second 1-D array.

Returns:
  An ee.Number.

methods.name: ee.ee_array.Array.double
documentation: On an element-wise basis, casts the input value to a 64-bit float.

methods.name: ee.ee_array.Array.eigen
documentation:
Computes the real eigenvectors and eigenvalues of a square 2D array.

Returns:
  An ee.Array with A rows and A+1 columns, where each row contains an
  eigenvalue in the first column, and the corresponding eigenvector in the
  remaining A columns. The rows are sorted by eigenvalue, in descending
  order. This implementation uses DecompositionFactory.eig() from
  http://ejml.org.

methods.name: ee.ee_array.Array.eq
documentation:
Returns 1 if the first value is equal to the second.

Args:
  right: The values to compare with.

Returns:
  An ee.Array.

methods.name: ee.ee_array.Array.erf
documentation: On an element-wise basis, computes the error function of the input.

methods.name: ee.ee_array.Array.erfInv
documentation: Computes the inverse error function of the input.

methods.name: ee.ee_array.Array.erfc
documentation: Computes the complementary error function of the input.

methods.name: ee.ee_array.Array.erfcInv
documentation: Computes the inverse complementary error function of the input.

methods.name: ee.ee_array.Array.exp
documentation: Computes Euler's number e raised to the power of the input.

methods.name: ee.ee_array.Array.first
documentation:
On an element-wise basis, selects the value of the first value.

Args:
  right: The matching values that are always ignored.

Returns:
  An ee.Array.

methods.name: ee.ee_array.Array.firstNonZero
documentation:
Selects the first value if it is non-zero, otherwise the second value.

Args:
  right: The values to use if the array has a zero in the corresponding
    position.

Returns:
  An ee.Array.

methods.name: ee.ee_array.Array.float
documentation: On an element-wise basis, casts the input value to a 32-bit float.

methods.name: ee.ee_array.Array.floor
documentation: Computes the largest integer less than or equal to the input.

methods.name: ee.ee_array.Array.gamma
documentation: On an element-wise basis, computes the gamma function of the input.

methods.name: ee.ee_array.Array.gammainc
documentation:
Calculates the regularized lower incomplete Gamma function γ(x,a).

Args:
  right: The a values for gammainc(x, a).

Returns:
  An ee.Array.

methods.name: ee.ee_array.Array.get
documentation:
Extracts the value at the given position from the input array.

Args:
  position: The coordinates of the element to get.

Returns:
  An ee.Number.

methods.name: ee.ee_array.Array.gt
documentation:
Returns 1 if the first value is greater than the second.

Args:
  right: The values to compare with.

Returns:
  An ee.Array.

methods.name: ee.ee_array.Array.gte
documentation:
Returns 1 if the first value is greater than or equal to the second.

Args:
  right: The values to compare with.

Returns:
  An ee.Array.

methods.name: ee.ee_array.Array.hypot
documentation:
Calculates the magnitude of the 2D vector [x, y].

Args:
  right: The y values.

Returns:
  An ee.Array.

methods.name: ee.ee_array.Array.identity
documentation:
Returns a 2D identity matrix of the given size.

Args:
  size: The length of each axis.

methods.name: ee.ee_array.Array.int
documentation: Casts the input value to a signed 32-bit integer.

methods.name: ee.ee_array.Array.int16
documentation: Casts the input value to a signed 16-bit integer.

methods.name: ee.ee_array.Array.int32
documentation: Casts the input value to a signed 32-bit integer.

methods.name: ee.ee_array.Array.int64
documentation: Casts the input value to a signed 64-bit integer.

methods.name: ee.ee_array.Array.int8
documentation: Casts the input value to a signed 8-bit integer.

methods.name: ee.ee_array.Array.lanczos
documentation: Computes the Lanczos approximation of the input.

methods.name: ee.ee_array.Array.leftShift
documentation:
On an element-wise basis, calculates the left shift of v1 by v2 bits.

Args:
  right: How many bits to shift by.

Returns:
  An ee.Array.

methods.name: ee.ee_array.Array.length
documentation: Returns a 1-D ee.Array containing the length of each dimension.

methods.name: ee.ee_array.Array.log
documentation: On an element-wise basis, computes the natural logarithm of the input.

methods.name: ee.ee_array.Array.log10
documentation: On an element-wise basis, computes the base-10 logarithm of the input.

methods.name: ee.ee_array.Array.long
documentation: Casts the input value to a signed 64-bit integer.

methods.name: ee.ee_array.Array.lt
documentation:
Returns 1 if the first value is less than the second.

Args:
  right: The values to compare with.

Returns:
  An ee.Array.

methods.name: ee.ee_array.Array.lte
documentation:
Returns 1 if the first value is less than or equal to the second.

Args:
  right: The values to compare with.

Returns:
  An ee.Array.

methods.name: ee.ee_array.Array.mask
documentation:
Creates an array of just the elements that have non-zero mask matches.

Creates a subarray by slicing out each position in an input array that is
parallel to a non-zero element of the given mask array.

Args:
  mask: Mask array.

Returns:
  An ee.Array.

methods.name: ee.ee_array.Array.matrixCholeskyDecomposition
documentation:
Calculates the Cholesky decomposition of a matrix.

The Cholesky decomposition is a decomposition into the form L * L' where L
is a lower triangular matrix. The input must be a symmetric
positive-definite matrix. Returns a dictionary with 1 entry named 'L'.

Returns:
  An ee.Dictionary.

methods.name: ee.ee_array.Array.matrixDeterminant
documentation: Computes the determinant of the matrix.

methods.name: ee.ee_array.Array.matrixDiagonal
documentation: Computes the diagonal of the matrix in a single column.

methods.name: ee.ee_array.Array.matrixFnorm
documentation: Computes the Frobenius norm of the matrix.

methods.name: ee.ee_array.Array.matrixInverse
documentation: Computes the inverse of the matrix.

methods.name: ee.ee_array.Array.matrixLUDecomposition
documentation:
Calculates the LU matrix decomposition.

The LU decomposition such that P×input=L×U, where L is lower triangular
(with unit diagonal terms), U is upper triangular and P is a partial pivot
permutation matrix.

The input matrix must be square. Returns a dictionary with entries named
'L', 'U' and 'P'.

Returns:
  An ee.Dictionary.

methods.name: ee.ee_array.Array.matrixMultiply
documentation:
Returns the matrix multiplication A * B.

Args:
  right: The right-hand value.

Returns:
  An ee.Array.

methods.name: ee.ee_array.Array.matrixPseudoInverse
documentation: Computes the Moore-Penrose pseudoinverse of the matrix.

methods.name: ee.ee_array.Array.matrixQRDecomposition
documentation:
Returns the QR decomposition of the input matrix.

Calculates the QR-decomposition of a matrix into two matrices Q and R such
that input = QR, where Q is orthogonal, and R is upper triangular.

Returns a dictionary with entries named 'Q' and 'R'.

Returns:
  An ee.Dictionary.

methods.name: ee.ee_array.Array.matrixSingularValueDecomposition
documentation:
Returns the Singular Value Decomposition of the input matrix.

Calculates the Singular Value Decomposition of the input matrix into U×S×V',
such that U and V are orthogonal and S is diagonal.

Returns:
  An ee.Dictionary with entries named 'U', 'S' and 'V'.

methods.name: ee.ee_array.Array.matrixSolve
documentation:
Returns the least-squares solution of the input matrix.

Solves for x in the matrix equation A * x = B, finding a least-squares
solution if A is overdetermined.

Args:
  right: The right-hand value.

Returns:
  An ee.Array.

methods.name: ee.ee_array.Array.matrixToDiag
documentation: Computes a square diagonal matrix from a single column matrix.

methods.name: ee.ee_array.Array.matrixTrace
documentation: Computes the trace of the matrix.

methods.name: ee.ee_array.Array.matrixTranspose
documentation:
Transposes two dimensions of an array.

Args:
  axis1: First axis to swap. Defaults to 0.
  axis2: Second axis to swap. Defaults to 1.

Returns:
  An ee.Array.

methods.name: ee.ee_array.Array.max
documentation:
Selects the maximum of the first and second values.

Args:
  right: The values to compare with.

Returns:
  An ee.Array.

methods.name: ee.ee_array.Array.min
documentation:
Selects the minimum of the first and second values.

Args:
  right: The values to compare with.

Returns:
  An ee.Array.

methods.name: ee.ee_array.Array.mod
documentation:
Calculates the remainder of the first value divided by the second.

Args:
  right: The values to divide by.

Returns:
  An ee.Array.

methods.name: ee.ee_array.Array.multiply
documentation:
On an element-wise basis, multiplies the first value by the second.

Args:
  right: The values to multiply by.

Returns:
  An ee.Array.

methods.name: ee.ee_array.Array.neq
documentation:
Returns 1 if the first value is not equal to the second.

Args:
  right: The right-hand value.

Returns:
  An ee.Array.

methods.name: ee.ee_array.Array.Not
documentation: Returns 0 if the input is non-zero, and 1 otherwise.

methods.name: ee.ee_array.Array.Or
documentation:
On an element-wise basis, returns 1 if either input value is non-zero.

Args:
  right: The right-hand value.

Returns:
  An ee.Array.

methods.name: ee.ee_array.Array.pad
documentation:
Pad an array to a given length.

The pad value will be repeatedly appended to the array to extend it to given
length along each axis. If the array is already as large or larger than a
given length, it will remain unchanged along that axis.

Args:
  lengths: A list of new lengths for each axis.
  pad: The value with which to pad the array. Defaults to 0.

Returns:
  An ee.Array.

methods.name: ee.ee_array.Array.pow
documentation:
On an element-wise basis, raises the first value to the power of the second.

Args:
  right: The right-hand value.

Returns:
  An ee.Array.

methods.name: ee.ee_array.Array.project
documentation:
Returns the projected array.

Projects an array to a lower dimensional space by specifying the axes to
retain. Dropped axes must be at most length 1.

Args:
  axes: The axes to project onto. Other axes will be discarded, and must be
    at most length 1.

Returns:
  An ee.Array.

methods.name: ee.ee_array.Array.reduce
documentation:
Returns the result of applying the reducer on the array.

Apply a reducer to an array by collapsing all the input values along each
specified axis into a single output value computed by the reducer.

The output always has the same dimensionality as the input, and the
individual axes are affected as follows:

- The axes specified in the 'axes' parameter have their length reduced to 1
  (by applying the reducer).
- If the reducer has multiple inputs or multiple outputs, the axis specified
  in 'fieldAxis' will be used to provide the reducer's inputs and store the
  reducer's outputs.
- All other axes are unaffected (independent reductions are performed).

Args:
  reducer: The reducer to apply. Each of its outputs must be a number, not
    an array or other type.
  axes: The list of axes over which to reduce. The output will have a length
    of 1 in all these axes.
  fieldAxis: The axis to use as the reducer's input and output fields. Only
    required if the reducer has multiple inputs or multiple outputs, in
    which case the axis must have length equal to the number of reducer
    inputs, and in the result it will have length equal to the number of
    reducer outputs.

Returns:
  An ee.Array.

methods.name: ee.ee_array.Array.repeat
documentation:
Repeats the array along the given axis.

The result will have the shape of the input, except length along the
repeated axis will be multiplied by the given number of copies.

Args:
  axis: The axis along which to repeat the array. Defaults to 0.
  copies: The number of copies of this array to concatenate along the given
    axis. Defaults to 2.

Returns:
  An ee.Array.

methods.name: ee.ee_array.Array.reshape
documentation:
Reshapes an array to a new list of dimension lengths.

Args:
  shape: New shape to which arrays are converted. If one component of the
    shape is the special value -1, the size of that dimension is computed so
    that the total size remains constant. In particular, a shape of [-1]
    flattens into 1-D. At most one component of shape can be -1.

Returns:
  An ee.Array.

methods.name: ee.ee_array.Array.rightShift
documentation:
Calculates the signed right shift of v1 by v2 bits.

Args:
  right: The values to shift by.

Returns:
  An ee.Array.

methods.name: ee.ee_array.Array.round
documentation: On an element-wise basis, computes the integer nearest to the input.

methods.name: ee.ee_array.Array.short
documentation: On an element-wise basis, casts the input value to a signed 16-bit integer.

methods.name: ee.ee_array.Array.signum
documentation:
Returns -1 or 1 base on the sign of the input or 0 if the input is zero.

On an element-wise basis, computes the signum function (sign) of the input;
The return value is 0 if the input is 0, 1 if the input is greater than 0,
-1 if the input is less than 0.

Returns:
  An ee.Array.

methods.name: ee.ee_array.Array.sin
documentation: On an element-wise basis, computes the sine of the input in radians.

methods.name: ee.ee_array.Array.sinh
documentation: On an element-wise basis, computes the hyperbolic sine of the input.

methods.name: ee.ee_array.Array.slice
documentation:
Returns the result of slicing the array along the given axis.

Creates a subarray by slicing out each position along the given axis from
the 'start' (inclusive) to 'end' (exclusive) by increments of 'step'.

The result will have as many dimensions as the input, and the same length in
all directions except the slicing axis, where the length will be the number
of positions from 'start' to 'end' by 'step' that are in range of the input
array's length along 'axis'. This means the result can be length 0 along the
given axis if start=end, or if the start or end values are entirely out of
range.

Args:
  axis: The axis to slice on. Defaults to 0.
  start: The coordinate of the first slice (inclusive) along 'axis'.
    Negative numbers are used to position the start of slicing relative to
    the end of the array, where -1 starts at the last position on the axis,
    -2 starts at the next to last position, etc.  Defaults to 0.
  end: The coordinate (exclusive) at which to stop taking slices. By default
    this will be the length of the given axis. Negative numbers are used to
    position the end of slicing relative to the end of the array, where -1
    will exclude the last position, -2 will exclude the last two positions,
    etc.
  step: The separation between slices along 'axis'; a slice will be taken at
    each whole multiple of 'step' from 'start' (inclusive) to 'end'
    (exclusive). Must be positive.  Defaults to 1.

Returns:
  An ee.Array.

methods.name: ee.ee_array.Array.sort
documentation:
Sorts elements of the array along one axis.

Args:
  keys: Optional keys to sort by. If not provided, the values are used as
    the keys. The keys can only have multiple elements along one axis, which
    determines the direction to sort in.

Returns:
  An ee.Array.

methods.name: ee.ee_array.Array.sqrt
documentation: On an element-wise basis, computes the square root of the input.

methods.name: ee.ee_array.Array.subtract
documentation:
On an element-wise basis, subtracts the second value from the first.

Args:
  right: The values to subtract.

Returns:
  An ee.Array.

methods.name: ee.ee_array.Array.tan
documentation: Computes the tangent of the input in radians.

methods.name: ee.ee_array.Array.tanh
documentation: Computes the hyperbolic tangent of the input.

methods.name: ee.ee_array.Array.toDouble

methods.name: ee.ee_array.Array.toFloat

methods.name: ee.ee_array.Array.transpose
documentation:
Transposes two dimensions of an array.

Args:
  axis1: First axis to swap. Defaults to 0.
  axis2: Second axis to swap. Defaults to 1.

Returns:
  An ee.Array.

methods.name: ee.ee_array.Array.trigamma
documentation: Computes the trigamma function of the input.

methods.name: ee.ee_array.Array.uint16
documentation: Casts the input value to an unsigned 16-bit integer.

methods.name: ee.ee_array.Array.uint32
documentation: Casts the input value to an unsigned 32-bit integer.

methods.name: ee.ee_array.Array.uint8
documentation: Casts the input value to an unsigned 8-bit integer.

An object to represent an Earth Engine Array.

Examples:
  # Requires an explicit PixelType if no data.
  ee.Array([], ee.PixelType.int8()); value: []
  ee.Array([[]], ee.PixelType.uint8()); value: [[]]
  ee.Array([[], []], ee.PixelType.float()); value: [[], []]
  # 1-D Arrays
  ee.Array([0]); value: [0]
  ee.Array([0, 1]); value: [0, 1]
  # 2-D Arrays
  ee.Array([[1]]); value: [[1]]
  ee.Array([[0, 1], [2, 3]]); value: [[0,1],[2,3]]
  # Arrays from ee.Number.
  ee.Array([ee.Number(123).toUint8()]); value: [123]
  # Lists are useful ways to construct larger Arrays.
  ee.Array(ee.List.sequence(0, 10, 2)); value: [0, 2, 4, 6, 8, 10]

modules.name: ee.function
documentation: A base class for EE Functions.

classes.name: ee.function.Function

methods.name: ee.function.Function._registerPromoter
documentation:
Registers a function used to type-coerce arguments and return values.

Args:
  promoter: A function used to type-coerce arguments and return values.
      Passed a value as the first parameter and a type name as the second.
      Can be used, for example, promote numbers or strings to Images.
      Should return the input promoted if the type is recognized,
      otherwise the original input.

methods.name: ee.function.Function.getSignature
documentation:
Returns a description of the interface provided by this function.

Returns:
  The function's signature, a dictionary containing:
    name: string
    returns: type name string
    args: list of argument dictionaries, each containing:
      name: string
      type: type name string
      optional: boolean
      default: an arbitrary primitive or encodable object

methods.name: ee.function.Function.call
documentation:
Calls the function with the given positional and keyword arguments.

Args:
  *args: The positional arguments to pass to the function.
  **kwargs: The named arguments to pass to the function.

Returns:
  A ComputedObject representing the called function. If the signature
  specifies a recognized return type, the returned value will be cast
  to that type.

methods.name: ee.function.Function.apply
documentation:
Calls the function with a dictionary of named arguments.

Args:
  named_args: A dictionary of named arguments to pass to the function.

Returns:
  A ComputedObject representing the called function. If the signature
  specifies a recognized return type, the returned value will be cast
  to that type.

methods.name: ee.function.Function.promoteArgs
documentation:
Promotes arguments to their types based on the function's signature.

Verifies that all required arguments are provided and no unknown arguments
are present.

Args:
  args: A dictionary of keyword arguments to the function.

Returns:
  A dictionary of promoted arguments.

Raises:
  EEException: If unrecognized arguments are passed or required ones are
      missing.

methods.name: ee.function.Function.nameArgs
documentation:
Converts a list of positional arguments to a map of keyword arguments.

Uses the function's signature for argument names. Note that this does not
check whether the array contains enough arguments to satisfy the call.

Args:
  args: Positional arguments to the function.
  extra_keyword_args: Optional named arguments to add.

Returns:
  Keyword arguments to the function.

Raises:
  EEException: If conflicting arguments or too many of them are supplied.

methods.name: ee.function.Function.__str__
documentation: Returns a user-readable docstring for this function.

An abstract base class for functions callable by the EE API.

Subclasses must implement encode_invocation() and getSignature().

classes.name: ee.function.SecondOrderFunction

methods.name: ee.function.SecondOrderFunction.__init__
documentation:
Creates a SecondOrderFunction.

Args:
  function_body: The function that returns the function to execute.
  signature: The signature of the function to execute, as described in
    getSignature().
A function that executes the result of a function.

modules.name: ee.customfunction
documentation: An object representing a custom EE Function.

classes.name: ee.customfunction.CustomFunction

methods.name: ee.customfunction.CustomFunction.__init__
documentation:
Creates a function defined by a given expression with unbound variables.

The expression is created by evaluating the given function
using variables as placeholders.

Args:
  signature: The function signature. If any of the argument names are
      null, their names will be generated deterministically, based on
      the body.
  body: The Python function to evaluate.

methods.name: ee.customfunction.CustomFunction.getSignature
documentation: Returns a description of the interface provided by this function.

methods.name: ee.customfunction.CustomFunction.variable
documentation:
Returns a placeholder variable with a given name and EE type.

Args:
  type_name: A class to mimic.
  name: The name of the variable as it will appear in the
      arguments of the custom functions that use this variable. If null,
      a name will be auto-generated in _resolveNamelessArgs().

Returns:
  A variable with the given name implementing the given type.

methods.name: ee.customfunction.CustomFunction.create
documentation:
Creates a CustomFunction.

The result calls a given native function with the specified return type and
argument types and auto-generated argument names.

Args:
  func: The native function to wrap.
  return_type: The type of the return value, either as a string or a
      class reference.
  arg_types: The types of the arguments, either as strings or class
      references.

Returns:
  The constructed CustomFunction.

methods.name: ee.customfunction.CustomFunction._resolveNamelessArgs
documentation:
Deterministically generates names for unnamed variables.

The names are based on the body of the function.

Args:
  signature: The signature which may contain null argument names.
  variables: A list of variables, some of which may be nameless.
      These will be updated to include names when this method returns.
  body: The Python function to evaluate.

Returns:
  The signature with null arg names resolved.
An object representing a custom EE Function.
Multiple inheritance is necessary because a CustomFunction needs to know how
to encode itself in different ways:
- as an Encodable: encode its definition
- as a Function: encode its invocation (which may also involve encoding its
definition, if that hasn't happened yet).

modules.name: ee.deserializer
documentation: A deserializer that decodes EE object trees from JSON DAGs.

functions.name: ee.deserializer.fromJSON
documentation:
Deserialize an object from a JSON string appropriate for API calls.

Args:

Returns:

functions.name: ee.deserializer.decode
documentation:
Decodes an object previously encoded using the EE API v2 (DAG) format.

Args:

Returns:

functions.name: ee.deserializer._decodeValue

Decodes an object previously encoded using the EE API v2 (DAG) format.

This uses a provided scope for ValueRef lookup and does not allow the
input to be a CompoundValue.

Args:

  named_values: The objects that can be referenced by ValueRefs.

Returns:
  The decoded object.

functions.name: ee.deserializer._invocation

functions.name: ee.deserializer.fromCloudApiJSON

Deserializes an object from the JSON string used in Cloud API calls.

Args:

Returns:
  The deserialized object.

functions.name: ee.deserializer.decodeCloudApi
documentation:
Decodes an object previously encoded using the EE Cloud API format.

Args:
  json_obj: The serialized object to decode.

Returns:
  The decoded object.

modules.name: ee.computedobject
documentation: A representation of an Earth Engine computed object.

classes.name: ee.computedobject.ComputedObjectMetaclass

methods.name: ee.computedobject.ComputedObjectMetaclass.__call__
documentation: Creates a computed object, catching self-casts.

A meta-class that makes type coercion idempotent.

If an instance of a ComputedObject subclass is instantiated by passing
another instance of that class as the sole argument, this short-circuits
and returns that argument.

classes.name: ee.computedobject.ComputedObject

methods.name: ee.computedobject.ComputedObject.__init__
documentation:
Creates a computed object.

Args:
  func: The ee.Function called to compute this object, either as an
    Algorithm name or an ee.Function object.
  args: A dictionary of arguments to pass to the specified function. Note
    that the caller is responsible for promoting the arguments to the
    correct types.
  varName: A variable name. If not None, the object will be encoded as a
    reference to a CustomFunction variable of this name, and both 'func' and
    'args' must be None. If all arguments are None, the object is considered
    an unnamed variable, and a name will be generated when it is included in
    an ee.CustomFunction.

methods.name: ee.computedobject.ComputedObject.getInfo
documentation:
Fetch and return information about this object.

Returns:
  The object can evaluate to anything.
pylint: disable-next=useless-parent-delegation

methods.name: ee.computedobject.ComputedObject.encode
documentation: Encodes the object in a format compatible with Serializer.

methods.name: ee.computedobject.ComputedObject.serialize
documentation:
Serialize this object into a JSON string.

Args:
  pretty: A flag indicating whether to pretty-print the JSON.
  for_cloud_api: Whether the encoding should be done for the Cloud API or
    the legacy API.

Returns:
  The serialized representation of this object.

methods.name: ee.computedobject.ComputedObject.__str__
documentation: Writes out the object in a human-readable form.

methods.name: ee.computedobject.ComputedObject.isVariable
documentation: Returns whether this computed object is a variable reference.

methods.name: ee.computedobject.ComputedObject.aside
documentation:
Calls a function passing this object as the first argument.

Returns the object itself for chaining. Convenient when debugging. For
example:

c = (ee.ImageCollection('foo').aside(logging.info)
         .filterDate('2001-01-01', '2002-01-01').aside(logging.info)
         .filterBounds(geom).aside(logging.info)
         .aside(addToMap, {'min': 0, 'max': 142})
         .select('a', 'b'))

Args:
  func: The function to call.
  *var_args: Any extra arguments to pass to the function.

Returns:
  The same object, for chaining.

methods.name: ee.computedobject.ComputedObject.name
documentation: Returns the name of the object, used in __str__().

methods.name: ee.computedobject.ComputedObject._cast
documentation:
Cast a ComputedObject to a new instance of the same class as this.

Args:
  obj: The object to cast.

Returns:
  The cast object, and instance of the class on which this method is called.

methods.name: ee.computedobject.ComputedObject.freeze
documentation: Freeze a list or dict so it can be hashed.

class_variables.name: ee.computedobject.ComputedObject.varName
documentation: pylint: disable=g-bad-name

A representation of an Earth Engine computed object.

This is a base class for most API objects.

The class itself is not abstract as it is used to wrap the return values of
algorithms that produce unrecognized types with the minimal functionality
necessary to interact well with the rest of the API.

ComputedObjects come in two flavors:
1. If func != null and args != null, the ComputedObject is encoded as an
   invocation of func with args.
2. If func == null and args == null, the ComputedObject is a variable
   reference. The variable name is stored in its varName member. Note that
   in this case, varName may still be null; this allows the name to be
   deterministically generated at a later time. This is used to generate
   deterministic variable names for mapped functions, ensuring that nested
   mapping calls do not use the same variable name.

modules.name: ee.ee_list
documentation: A wrapper for lists.

classes.name: ee.ee_list.List

methods.name: ee.ee_list.List.__init__
documentation:
Construct a list wrapper.

This constructor accepts the following args:
  1) A bare list.
  2) A ComputedObject returning a list.

Args:
  arg: The list to wrap.

Raises:
  ee_exception.EEException: On bad input.

methods.name: ee.ee_list.List.repeat
documentation:
Returns a new list containing value repeated count times.

Args:
  value: The value to repeat.
  count: The number of times to repeat the value.

methods.name: ee.ee_list.List.sequence
documentation:
Returns a List of numbers from start to end (inclusive).

Generate a sequence of numbers from start to end (inclusive) in increments
of step, or in count equally-spaced increments. If end is not specified it
is computed from start + step * count, so at least one of end or count must
be specified.

Args:
  start: The starting number.
  end: The ending number.
  step: The increment.
  count: The number of increments.

methods.name: ee.ee_list.List.initialize
documentation: Imports API functions to this class.

methods.name: ee.ee_list.List.reset
documentation: Removes imported API functions from this class.

methods.name: ee.ee_list.List.add
documentation:
Appends the element to the end of list.

Args:
  element: The object to add.

Returns:
  An ee.List with the element added to the end.

methods.name: ee.ee_list.List.cat
documentation:
Concatenates the contents of other onto list.

Args:
  other: Another list to add to the end of the list.

Returns:
  An ee.List with the elements of the original list followed by the elements
  of other.

methods.name: ee.ee_list.List.contains
documentation:
Returns true if list contains element.

Args:
  element: An object to test for presence in the list.

Returns:
  Returns a Boolean ComputedObject. True if the list has the element. False
  if the element is not in the list.

methods.name: ee.ee_list.List.containsAll
documentation:
Returns true if list contains all of the elements of other.

The results are independent of the order.

Args:
  other: A list of elements to check for presence in the list.

Returns:
  Boolean ComputedObject. True if the list has all of the elements of other.

methods.name: ee.ee_list.List.distinct
documentation: Returns a copy of list without duplicate elements.

methods.name: ee.ee_list.List.equals
documentation:
Returns true if the list contains in order the same elements as other.

Args:
  other: List to compare to.

Returns:
  Boolean ComputedObject.

methods.name: ee.ee_list.List.filter
documentation:
Filters a list to only the elements that match the given filter.

To filter list items that aren't images or features, test a property
named 'item', e.g., ee.Filter.gt('item', 3).

Args:
  filter: The ee.Filter instance to apply.

Returns:
  An ee.List with only the elements that pass the filter.
pylint: disable-next=redefined-builtin

methods.name: ee.ee_list.List.flatten
documentation: Flattens any sublists into a single list.

methods.name: ee.ee_list.List.frequency
documentation:
Returns the number of elements in list equal to element.

Args:
  element: The value to match against.

Returns:
  Returns the count of elements that match element.

methods.name: ee.ee_list.List.get
documentation:
Returns the element at the specified position in list.

Args:
  index: Offset from where to get the element. A negative index counts
    backwards from the end of the list.

Returns:
  An ee.ComputedObject.

methods.name: ee.ee_list.List.getArray
documentation:
Returns the array at the specified position in list.

If the value is not a array, an error will occur.

Args:
  index: Offset from where to get the element. A negative index counts
    backwards from the end of the list.

Returns:
  An ee.Array.

methods.name: ee.ee_list.List.getGeometry
documentation:
Returns the geometry at the specified position in list.

If the value is not a geometry, an error will occur.

Args:
  index: Offset from where to get the element. A negative index counts
    backwards from the end of the list.

Returns:
  An ee.Geometry.

methods.name: ee.ee_list.List.getNumber
documentation:
Returns the number at the specified position in list.

If the value is not a number, an error will occur.

Args:
  index: Offset from where to get the element. A negative index counts
    backwards from the end of the list.

Returns:
  An ee.Number.

methods.name: ee.ee_list.List.getString
documentation:
Returns the string at the specified position in list.

If the value is not a string, an error will occur.

Args:
  index: Offset from where to get the element. A negative index counts
    backwards from the end of the list.

Returns:
  An ee.String.

methods.name: ee.ee_list.List.indexOf
documentation:
Returns the position of the first occurrence of element.

Returns the position of the first occurrence of target in list, or -1 if
list does not contain the target.

Args:
  element: ComputedObject to search for.

Returns:
  An integer as an ee.Number.

methods.name: ee.ee_list.List.indexOfSublist
documentation:
Returns the position of the first occurrence of target.

Returns the starting position of the first occurrence of target within list,
or -1 if there is no such occurrence.

Args:
  target: An ee.List to search for.

Returns:
  An integer as an ee.Number.

methods.name: ee.ee_list.List.insert
documentation:
Inserts element at the specified position in list.

A negative index counts backwards from the end of the list.

Args:
  index: Offset from where to get the element. A negative index counts
    backwards from the end of the list.
  element: A ComputedObject to insert.

Returns:
  An ee.List.

methods.name: ee.ee_list.List.iterate
documentation:
Iterate an algorithm over a list.

The algorithm is expected to take two objects, the current list item, and
the result from the previous iteration or the value of first for the first
iteration.

Args:
  function: A function to apply to the list.
  first: The first item to pass the function.

Returns:
  An ee.List.
TODO: Improve the type of `function`

methods.name: ee.ee_list.List.join
documentation:
Returns a string with the list elements with the separator between them.

Returns a string containing the elements of the list joined together with
the specified separator between elements.

Note: The string form of list elements which are not strings, numbers, or
booleans is currently not well-defined and subject to change.

Args:
  separator: A string to but between elements.

Returns:
  An ee.String.

methods.name: ee.ee_list.List.lastIndexOfSubList
documentation:
Returns the position of that last instance of target in the list.

Returns the starting position of the last occurrence of target within list,
or -1 if there is no such occurrence.

Args:
  target: A list to search for.

Returns:
  An integer as an ee.Number.

methods.name: ee.ee_list.List.length
documentation: Returns the number of elements in list.

methods.name: ee.ee_list.List.map
documentation:
Map an algorithm over a list.

The algorithm is expected to take an Object and return an Object.

Args:
  baseAlgorithm: The function to apply.
  dropNulls: If true, the mapped algorithm is allowed to return nulls, and
    the elements for which it returns nulls will be dropped.

Returns:
  An ee.List.
TODO: Improve the type of `baseAlgorithm`.

methods.name: ee.ee_list.List.reduce
documentation:
Apply a reducer to a list.

If the reducer takes more than 1 input, then each element in the list is
assumed to be a list of inputs. If the reducer returns a single output, it
is returned directly, otherwise returns a dictionary containing the named
reducer outputs.

Args:
  reducer: An ee.Reducer instance.

Returns:
  Return depends on the specific reducer used.

methods.name: ee.ee_list.List.remove
documentation:
Removes the first occurrence of the specified element from list.

Args:
  element: The item to remove from the list.

Returns:
  An ee.List.

methods.name: ee.ee_list.List.removeAll
documentation:
Removes from list all of the elements that are contained in other list.

Args:
  other: A list of the elements to remove.

Returns:
  An ee.List.

methods.name: ee.ee_list.List.replace
documentation:
Replaces the first occurrence of oldval in list with newval.

Args:
  oldval: The value to be replaced.
  newval: The value to be put in place of oldval.

Returns:
  An ee.List.

methods.name: ee.ee_list.List.replaceAll
documentation:
Replaces all occurrences of oldval in list with newval.

Args:
  oldval: The value to be replaced.
  newval: The value to be put in place of oldval.

Returns:
  An ee.List.

methods.name: ee.ee_list.List.reverse
documentation: Reverses the order of the elements in list.

methods.name: ee.ee_list.List.rotate
documentation:
Rotates the elements of the list by the specified distance.

Elements rotated off the end are pushed onto the other end of the list.

Args:
  distance: How many positions to shift all the values.

Returns:
  An ee.List.

methods.name: ee.ee_list.List.set
documentation:
Sets the value at the specified position in list.

Replaces the value at the specified position in list with element. A
negative index counts backwards from the end of the list.

Args:
  index: Offset from where to get the element. A negative index counts
    backwards from the end of the list.
  element: A ComputedObject to set at the position of index.

Returns:
  An ee.List.

methods.name: ee.ee_list.List.shuffle
documentation:
Randomly permute the specified list.

Note that the permutation order will always be the same for any given seed,
unless the value for seed is false.

Args:
  seed: A long integer to use as a seed for the randomization. If the
    boolean value of false is passed, then a completely random and
    unreproducible order will be generated.

Returns:
  An ee.List.

methods.name: ee.ee_list.List.size
documentation: Returns the number of elements in list.

methods.name: ee.ee_list.List.slice
documentation:
Returns a range of elements from a list.

Negative values for start or end count backwards from the end of the list.
Values greater than the size of the list are valid but are truncated to the
size of list.

For start and end, a negative index counts backwards from the end of the
list.

Args:
  start: Offset from where to get the elements (inclusive).
  end: Offset from where to stop getting the elements (exclusive).
  step: How many elements to move forward to get the next element.

Returns:
  An ee.List.

methods.name: ee.ee_list.List.sort
documentation:
Sorts the list into ascending order.

If the keys argument is provided, then it is sorted first, and the
elements of list are placed in the same order.

Args:
  keys: Optional keys to sort by. If keys is provided, it must have the
    same length as list.

Returns:
  An ee.List.

methods.name: ee.ee_list.List.splice
documentation:
Removes elements from list and replaces with elements from other.

Args:
  start: Offset from where to begin getting elements. A negative index
    counts backwards from the end of the list.
  count: How many elements to replace.
  other: Elements to put in at the splice location.

Returns:
  An ee.List.

methods.name: ee.ee_list.List.swap
documentation:
Swaps the elements at the specified positions.

A negative position counts backwards from the end of the list.

Args:
  pos1: Offset of one element.
  pos2: Offset of the second element.

Returns:
  An ee.List.

methods.name: ee.ee_list.List.unzip
documentation:
Rearranges a list of lists.

Transposes a list of lists, extracting the first element of each inner list
into one list, the second elements into another, etc., up to the length of
the shortest inner list. The remaining items are discarded. The result is a
list of lists.

Returns:
  An ee.List.

methods.name: ee.ee_list.List.zip
documentation:
Pairs the elements of two lists to create a list of two-element lists.

When the input lists are of different sizes, the final list has the same
size as the shortest one.

Args:
  other: The list to merge into the current list.

Returns:
  An ee.List with two elements that are both ee.Lists.
An object to represent lists.

modules.name: ee.oauth
documentation:
Earth Engine OAuth2 helper functions for generating client tokens.

Typical use-case consists of:
1. Calling 'get_authorization_url'
2. Using a browser to access the output URL and copy the generated OAuth2 code
3. Calling 'request_token' to request a token using that code and the OAuth API
4. Calling 'write_private_json' to save the token at the path given by
   'get_credentials_path'

functions.name: ee.oauth.get_authorization_url
documentation: Returns a URL to generate an auth code.

functions.name: ee.oauth.request_token
documentation: Uses authorization code to request tokens.

functions.name: ee.oauth.write_private_json
documentation: Attempts to write the passed token to the given user directory.

functions.name: ee.oauth.in_colab_shell
documentation: Tests if the code is being executed within Google Colab.

functions.name: ee.oauth._in_jupyter_shell
documentation: Tests if the code is being executed within Jupyter.

functions.name: ee.oauth._project_number_from_client_id
documentation: Returns the project number associated with the given OAuth client ID.

functions.name: ee.oauth._obtain_and_write_token
documentation: Obtains and writes credentials token based on an authorization code.

functions.name: ee.oauth._display_auth_instructions_for_noninteractive
documentation: Displays instructions for authenticating without blocking for user input.

functions.name: ee.oauth._display_auth_instructions_with_print
documentation: Displays instructions for authenticating using a print statement.

functions.name: ee.oauth._display_auth_instructions_with_html
documentation: Displays instructions for authenticating using HTML code.

functions.name: ee.oauth._base64param
documentation: Encodes bytes for use as a URL parameter.

functions.name: ee.oauth._nonce_table
documentation: Makes random nonces, and adds PKCE challenges for each _verifier nonce.

functions.name: ee.oauth._open_new_browser
documentation: Opens a web browser if possible, returning True when so.

functions.name: ee.oauth._load_gcloud_credentials
documentation: Initializes credentials by running gcloud flows.

functions.name: ee.oauth._start_server
documentation: Starts and returns a web server that handles the OAuth callback.

functions.name: ee.oauth.authenticate
documentation:
Prompts the user to authorize access to Earth Engine via OAuth2.

Args:
  cli_authorization_code: An optional authorization code.  Supports CLI mode,
    where the code is passed as an argument to `earthengine authenticate`.
  quiet: If true, do not require interactive prompts and force --no-browser
    mode for gcloud-legacy. If false, never supply --no-browser. Default is
    None, which autodetects the --no-browser setting.
  cli_code_verifier: PKCE verifier to prevent auth code stealing.  Must be
    provided if cli_authorization_code is given.
  auth_mode: The authorization mode.  One of:
      "colab" - use the Colab authentication flow.
      "notebook" - send user to notebook authenticator page. Intended for
        web users who do not run code locally. Credentials expire in 7 days.
      "gcloud" - use gcloud to obtain credentials.
      "localhost" - sends credentials to the Python environment on the same
        localhost as the browser. Does not work for remote shells. Default
        port is 8085; use localhost:N set port or localhost:0 to auto-select.
      "gcloud-legacy" - use less convenient gcloud mode, for users without
        cloud projects.
      "appdefault" - included for legacy compatibility but not necessary.
        ee.Initialize() will always check for application default credentials.
      None - a default mode is chosen based on your environment.
 scopes: List of scopes to use for authorization. Defaults to [
   'https://www.googleapis.com/auth/earthengine',
   'https://www.googleapis.com/auth/devstorage.full_control' ].
 force: Will force authentication even if valid credentials already exist.

Returns:
  True if we found valid credentials and didn't run the auth flow, or
  otherwise None.

Raises:
   Exception: on invalid arguments.

variables.name: ee.oauth.REDIRECT_URI
documentation: Prompts user to copy-paste code

methods.name: ee.dictionary.Dictionary.combine
documentation:
Combines two dictionaries.

In the case of duplicate key names, the output will contain the value of the

dictionaries are ignored / removed.

Args:

Returns:

  values: A list of values.
TODO: keys should be a _StringListType.

methods.name: ee.dictionary.Dictionary.get
documentation:
Extracts a named value from a dictionary.

If the dictionary does not contain the given key, then defaultValue is
returned, unless it is null.

Args:

  defaultValue: The value to return if the key is not found.

Returns:

Extracts a named array value from a dictionary.

Extracts a named geometry value from a dictionary.

methods.name: ee.dictionary.Dictionary.keys
documentation: Retrieve the keys of a dictionary as a list.

methods.name: ee.dictionary.Dictionary.map
documentation:
Map an algorithm over a dictionary.

The algorithm is expected to take 2 arguments, a key from the existing
dictionary and the value it corresponds to, and return a new value for the
given key. If the algorithm returns null, the key is dropped.

Args:
  baseAlgorithm: A function taking key, value and returning the new value.

Returns:
  An ee.Dictionary with new values for each key.
pylint: disable-next=invalid-name

methods.name: ee.dictionary.Dictionary.remove
documentation:
Returns a dictionary with the specified keys removed.

Args:

methods.name: ee.dictionary.Dictionary.rename

Rename elements in a dictionary.

Args:

    Must have the same length as the 'from' list.
  overwrite: Allow overwriting existing properties with the same name.

Returns:
  An ee.Dictionary.
TODO: Make a tighter method signature.
pylint: disable-next=g-doc-args

methods.name: ee.dictionary.Dictionary.select
documentation:
Returns a dictionary with only the specified keys.

Args:

  ignoreMissing: Ignore selectors that don't match at least 1 key.
    Defaults to false.

Returns:
  An ee.Dictionary.

methods.name: ee.dictionary.Dictionary.set
documentation:
Set a value in a dictionary.

Args:

  value: The value to set for the key.

Returns:
  An ee.Dictionary.

methods.name: ee.dictionary.Dictionary.size
documentation: Returns the number of entries in a dictionary.

methods.name: ee.dictionary.Dictionary.toArray
documentation:
Returns numeric values of a dictionary as an array.

If no keys are specified, all values are returned in the natural ordering of
the dictionary's keys. The default 'axis' is 0.

Args:
  keys: An optional list of keys to subselect.
  axis: How to interpret values that are ee.Arrays. Defaults to 0.

Returns:
  An ee.Array.

methods.name: ee.dictionary.Dictionary.toImage
documentation:
Creates an image of constants from values in a dictionary.

The bands of the image are ordered and named according to the names
argument. If no names are specified, the bands are sorted
alpha-numerically.

Args:
  names: The order of the output bands.

Returns:
  An ee.Image.

methods.name: ee.dictionary.Dictionary.values
documentation:
Returns the values of a dictionary as a list.

If no keys are specified, all values are returned in the natural ordering of
the dictionary's keys.

Args:
  keys: An optional list of keys to subselect.

Returns:
  An ee.Array.
An object to represent dictionaries.

modules.name: ee.projection
documentation: A wrapper for Projections.

classes.name: ee.projection.Projection

methods.name: ee.projection.Projection.__init__
documentation:
Creates a Projection wrapper.

Returns a Projection with the given base coordinate system and the given
transform between projected coordinates and the base.

Args:
  crs: The base coordinate reference system of this Projection, given as a
    well-known authority code (e.g., 'EPSG:4326') or a WKT string.
  transform: The transform between projected coordinates and the base
    coordinate system, specified as a 2x3 affine transform matrix in
    row-major order: [xScale, xShearing, xTranslation, yShearing, yScale,
    yTranslation]. May not specify both this and 'transformWkt'.
  transformWkt: The transform between projected coordinates and the base
    coordinate system, specified as a WKT string. May not specify both this
    and 'transform'.

methods.name: ee.projection.Projection.initialize
documentation: Imports API functions to this class.

methods.name: ee.projection.Projection.reset
documentation: Removes imported API functions from this class.

methods.name: ee.projection.Projection.atScale
documentation:
Returns an ee.Projection scaled to the given scale in linear meters.

Returns the projection scaled such that its units have the given scale in
linear meters, as measured at the point of true scale.

Args:
  meters: The scale in linear meters.

methods.name: ee.projection.Projection.crs
documentation:
Returns the coordinate reference system (crs) of this projection.

Returns the authority code (e.g., 'EPSG:4326') for the base coordinate
system of this projection, or null if the base coordinate system is not
found in any available database.

methods.name: ee.projection.Projection.nominalScale
documentation:
Returns the linear scale in meters of the units of this projection.

Done as measured at the point of true scale.

methods.name: ee.projection.Projection.scale
documentation:
Returns an ee.Projection scaled by the given amount in each axis.

Args:
  x: The amount to scale by in the x axis.
  y: The amount to scale by in the y axis.

methods.name: ee.projection.Projection.transform
documentation:
Returns a WKT representation of the transform of this Projection.

This is the transform that converts from projected coordinates to the base
coordinate system.

methods.name: ee.projection.Projection.translate
documentation:
Returns an ee.Projection translated by the given amount in each axis.

Args:
  x: The amount to translate by in the x axis.
  y: The amount to translate by in the y axis.

methods.name: ee.projection.Projection.wkt
documentation: Returns a WKT representation of the base coordinate system of this Projection.

An object to represent an Earth Engine Projection.

Examples:
  # Construct a variety of projections.
  # crs: EPSG:4326, transform: identity
  ee.Projection('EPSG:4326')
  # crs: EPSG:4326, transform: projection.scale(2, 3)
  ee.Projection('EPSG:4326', [2, 0, 0, 0, 3, 0])
  # crs: EPSG:4326, transform wkt:
  ee.Projection(
      'EPSG:4326',
      transformWkt=(
          'GEOGCS["WGS 84",'
          '  DATUM["WGS_1984",'
          '    SPHEROID["WGS 84",6378137,298.257223563,'
          '      AUTHORITY["EPSG","7030"]],'
          '    AUTHORITY["EPSG","6326"]],'
          '  PRIMEM["Greenwich",0,'
          '    AUTHORITY["EPSG","8901"]],'
          '  UNIT["degree",0.0174532925199433,'
          '    AUTHORITY["EPSG","9122"]],'
          '  AXIS["Longitude",EAST],'
          '  AXIS["Latitude",NORTH]]'
      ),
  )
